v0.1.7:
— Updates tab: changelog text now follows the UI language (EN/RU). Both lists re-render on the fly as soon as you switch.
— Updates tab: the single «Changelog» section is split in two — app releases on the left, quick-patch on the right. Each block has its own «All / Important» filter.
— Updates tab: quick-patch no longer fails with «Checksum mismatch» — manifest hashes are now recomputed automatically on every commit.
— Dashboard: a new «Find Config» card shows whether the game is found in Steam and whether it has an `autoexec.cfg`. When the file is missing, a «Create autoexec.cfg» button appears and creates it.
v0.1.6:
— EN/RU localization: new `src/i18n.ts` module with a UI dictionary (sidebar/nav, dashboard, profile bar, game status, Visuals/Profiles/Updates pages, modals and updater messages). Deadlock feature names (Auto Parry, ESP, Slide Enhancer, FOV, etc.) intentionally remain in English.
— Strings are swapped via `data-i18n` / `data-i18n-attr` attributes on markup, plus an `i18n:change` event for dynamic texts (updater status, game status). The selected language is stored in `localStorage` (key `dl-lang`).
— Custom `<select>` on the Updates page: the native `<select>` stays in the DOM as the source of truth for value and events, with a HUD-styled trigger + listbox rendered on top. Keyboard navigation (↑/↓/Enter/Esc) and a `MutationObserver` sync so external option re-renders (profiles) don't lose the selection.
— Native Windows titlebar now matches the app palette: DWM API immersive dark mode, caption color `#0a0a0a` (matching the window background), muted text, a faint greenish border `#0d130d`. Mica/Acrylic is disabled so the caption color is applied literally without tinting.
— Removed the light theme toggle (`dl-theme`) and its `index.html` init — the app now uses a single dark theme.
— Large `src/styles.css` refactor (≈ +1500 lines): unified cards, states, dropdowns, updated sidebar/header markup — no visual regressions on existing screens.
— Dependencies: added `windows-sys 0.59` (features `Win32_Foundation`, `Win32_Graphics_Dwm`), windows target only.
v0.1.5:
Performance (before → after):
— Main window startup: 3–4 separate `invoke` calls (version, game status, quick-patch CSS, occasionally a duplicate version) → single `ui_startup_snapshot` — fewer IPC round trips, faster first paint.
— Game process polling: fixed `setInterval` every 2.8 s even in the background → adaptive timer: ~2.8 s when the tab is active, ~30 s when the window is minimized/hidden — less CPU and `tasklist` load.
— Visuals tab: comparison images were validated and loaded at every startup → deferred load on first open of Visuals — faster cold start.
— Compare slider: DOM updates on every `input` → coalesced through `requestAnimationFrame` — fewer redundant repaints while dragging.
— Navigation: repeated `querySelectorAll` per click → cached panel/menu node lists — fewer DOM walks.
— Vite build: `build.target: es2022` — less transpilation in the bundle from the same sources.
— Visuals Compare modal: HUD styling — glass, gradient, backdrop blur, neon border and glow, a bottom OFF/ON bar with indicator, entrance animation (compare logic unchanged).
v0.1.4:
— Windows auto-update: NSIS and WebView2 in an explicit silent configuration — `updater.windows.installMode: quiet`, WebView2 bootstrapper with `silent: true`, per-user install (`currentUser`), no installer language prompt.
— CI: release publish permissions (workflow read/write) + Tauri download cache for faster builds.
— Verification release after fixing GitHub Actions permissions.
v0.1.3:
— «What's new» and quick-patch changelog texts rewritten: bullet points describing the actual UI/logic changes, without generic build phrasing.
— GitHub release page description is now sourced from this file for the matching tag.
— Windows distribution is NSIS-only (`.exe`); MSI is disabled — single installer, simpler updates, shorter release prep.
v0.1.2:
— Windows update mode: `installMode: quiet` — the installer during auto-update no longer shows a window (NSIS runs in the background).
— Caveat: quiet mode does not request admin rights; if the app is installed machine-wide, the update may require a manual install or a per-user reinstall.
v0.1.1:
— Updates tab: the «What's new» list no longer widens the window when switching between «releases» / «quick-patch» — `min-width: auto` removed from grid cells, text wraps (`overflow-wrap`).
— Right column with the changelog stretches to the full available width.
— Game process check: `tasklist` invoked without a popup console window (`CREATE_NO_WINDOW`).
— Bundle now ships only Rework version notes; old Electron-era entries were removed from the changelog.
v0.1.0: — First public Rework on Tauri 2: main and splash windows, profiles (JSON in `%AppData%`), update checks and install, quick-patch channel from GitHub, settings and dashboard UI.
